chapter: Chapter: Civilising the Native, Educating the Nation

questions.question: What were the main institutions of indigenous education in India?
questions.answer: Pathshalas, gurukuls, and madrassas.

questions.question: Why did British officials criticize the indigenous education system?
questions.answer: They believed it lacked modern scientific knowledge.

questions.question: What was the main issue in the Orientalist–Anglicist debate?
questions.answer: Whether education should focus on Indian languages or English and Western knowledge.

questions.question: What did the Orientalists want to promote in Indian education?
questions.answer: Indian languages and classical texts.

questions.question: What did the Anglicists support in education policy?
questions.answer: English education and Western knowledge.

questions.question: What was the significance of Macaulay’s Minute?
questions.answer: It promoted English as the medium of education.

questions.question: What was Macaulay’s opinion about Indian literature?
questions.answer: He considered Western literature superior.

questions.question: Why did the British introduce English education in India?
questions.answer: To create a class of Indians who could assist in administration.

questions.question: Which document laid the foundation for modern education in India?
questions.answer: Wood’s Despatch of 1854.

questions.question: What major recommendation did Wood’s Despatch make?
questions.answer: Establishment of universities and teacher training institutions.

questions.question: Which universities were established in 1857?
questions.answer: Calcutta, Bombay, and Madras.

questions.question: How did English education influence Indian society?
questions.answer: It created an educated middle class.

questions.question: Why did traditional schools decline during colonial rule?
questions.answer: Because British policies favored English education.

questions.question: What ideas were introduced through Western education?
questions.answer: Ideas of liberty, equality, and democracy.

questions.question: How did education help social reform movements?
questions.answer: It spread awareness and new ideas.

questions.question: How did modern education influence the freedom movement?
questions.answer: It increased political awareness among Indians.

questions.question: What role did the educated middle class play in India?
questions.answer: They led social reforms and nationalist movements.

questions.question: What was the main medium of higher education during British rule?
questions.answer: English.

questions.question: Why did some Indians oppose colonial education policies?
questions.answer: Because they ignored Indian traditions and culture.

questions.question: What type of knowledge did Western education promote?
questions.answer: Scientific and modern knowledge.

questions.question: What was one long-term effect of colonial education?
questions.answer: Establishment of modern universities in India.

questions.question: How did education influence political thought in India?
questions.answer: It introduced democratic and nationalist ideas.

questions.question: Which Indian reformer supported modern education?
questions.answer: Raja Rammohan Roy.

questions.question: What was the aim of colonial education policy?
questions.answer: To spread Western knowledge and assist colonial administration.

questions.question: What is the lasting legacy of colonial education in India?
questions.answer: The modern education system with universities and structured curricula.
